title: achillea crustata
intro: Achillea crustata, also known as Yarrow or Milfoil, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its fern-like foliage and small white or yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and relieving menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Achillea crustata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Achillea crustata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
